content_type: Academic presentation on competency credentialing in flexible GI endoscopy
presenter: John D. Mellinger, MD, FACS, Professor and Chair of General Surgery, Southern Illinois University
key_topics_covered: SAGES, ASGE, ABCRS, and AAFP recommendations for procedure volumes
Therapeutic and advanced endoscopy procedure requirements
Correlation between procedure volume and technical competence
ERCP, EGD, colonoscopy, and flexible sigmoidoscopy training standards
Learning curves in endoscopic procedures (GAGES-UE and GAGES-C scores)
2007 ASGE guidelines on procedure numbers as minimum thresholds
Rural vs. urban surgeon endoscopy procedural volume disparities
RRC-S category requirements for endoscopy credentials (2009 onwards)
Importance of observed competence, cognitive base, and technical skill
reason_for_no_score: This is an educational/instructional lecture presentation on competency frameworks and credentialing standards for endoscopy training. It is not a performance video of an FLS task. No actual procedural performance, timing, or technical execution is demonstrated.

teaching_content_extracted.educational_framework: Competence determination requires: (1) volume of experience (breadth), (2) observed competence (peer review), (3) cognitive base (anatomy, physiology, pathophysiology, indications, complications, management, alternatives), and (4) technical skill (psychomotor, visual recognition, diagnostic and therapeutic capability)
volume_vs_competence_evidence: Esophageal intubation: 90% proficiency after 100 EGDs (Cass et al., Ann Intern Med 1993)
ERCP learning curve plateau at approximately 40-50 procedures (Watkins et al., Gastrointest Endosc 1996)
GAGES-UE scores reach plateau at approximately 50 procedures (Vassiliou et al., AJS 2010)
GAGES-C scores reach plateau at approximately 75 procedures
Retrograde cholangiography: 85% success rate at 70 cases with >100 cases needed for parallel selective biliary success (prospective evaluation)
180 cases required prospectively for 80% cannulation rate of desired duct
teaching_content_extracted.critical_asge_2007_position: Published required numbers represent an estimate and minimum threshold. Most trainees will require more (never less) than stated numbers.
proctoring_and_credentialing_requirement: Local and institutional responsibility with uniform standards
Proficiency attested by observed and documented performance from program director, service chief, or prior credentialing committee
Continuing education and performance monitoring for maintenance of privileges
Multidisciplinary committee assessment including complications, tissue review, and utilization
procedural_breakdown_examples: EUS: submucosal 40, pancreaticobiliary 75, FNA pancreatic 25, FNA nonpancreatic 25
teaching_content_extracted.rural_surgeon_implications: Rural surgeons perform significantly higher volumes of general endoscopy (220 vs 77 procedures/year, p<0.0001) but lower volumes of advanced procedures; highlights disparities in specialist access and training opportunities

confidence_rationale: Content is clearly an academic lecture with slides, citations, and educational framework. No FLS task performance is present.
